taxonomy: Bafodeya benna is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to West Africa, specifically in the tropical regions of the continent. The plant is also known as the African teak or Benin teak due to its resemblance to the Asian teak tree. Bafodeya benna is a deciduous tree that grows up to 30 meters tall, with a broad, rounded crown and smooth, gray bark. Its leaves are compound and have a distinctive shape, consisting of three to five leaflets that are dark green in color.
